goal: Add a side-effect-free manual PR/review receipt normalizer that converts operator-supplied PR metadata into BPK-012 review records and quarantines invalid receipts. Do not call GitHub, create PRs, merge branches, change workflows, add package changes, or touch runtime/frontend behavior.
scope_out: Do not call GitHub APIs, create PRs, merge branches, push to main, or change workflows.
Do not add package scripts, dependencies, environment variables, deployment notes, runtime routes, provider calls, database writes, GitHub writes, or frontend files.
claims: Operator-supplied PR review receipts can be normalized without GitHub side effects.
Invalid PR URLs, invalid task IDs, missing commits, invalid review decisions, and non-green check statuses are quarantined.
Accepted receipts are BPK-012-compatible review records.
The normalizer is side-effect-free and tested independently.
assumptions: The operator may paste PR metadata manually before a live GitHub connector exists.
Actual GitHub connector behavior belongs to a later block.
dod: builder/src/bpkPrReviewManualReceipts.ts exports side-effect-free receipt types and normalizer.
The normalizer returns accepted BPK-012 records and quarantined receipts with deterministic reasons.
builder/tests/bpkPrReviewManualReceipts.test.ts covers accepted receipt, invalid PR URL, invalid decision, failing/missing check, duplicate task, and no-mutation behavior.
review-packets/BPK-016.md records scope, safety invariants, gates, and verification.
The focused manual receipt test and npm run typecheck in builder/ pass.
stop_conditions: A GitHub API call, PR creation, merge, push, workflow change, runtime change, package change, deployment change, or frontend file becomes necessary.
prior_task_findings: BPK-012 evaluates supplied PR review metadata. BPK-016 should safely normalize manual receipts into that shape without GitHub side effects.
